palette_hint: Warm off-white background #FDF6EC, dark charcoal text #2C2C2C, burnt-sienna accent #B84C1E for headings and rule lines, muted sage #7A8C6E for secondary labels.
type_style: Georgia serif for headings at font-weight 700 for a warm editorial feel, system-ui sans-serif for body text at font-weight 400, comfortable 1.7 line-height throughout.
description: Ember & Rye Kitchen is a single-recipe showcase site presenting a slow-braised short rib with smoked rye whiskey glaze, laid out as a beautifully typeset digital recipe card across five focused pages.
constraints: All content centered in a single column, max-width 620px, with horizontal padding of 24px on each side.
Thin 1px horizontal rules in burnt-sienna #B84C1E separate every major section, with 32px vertical margin above and below each rule.
No images of any kind — all visual interest comes from typography, spacing, and solid color accents.
Page background is always #FDF6EC; each page has a small bold page-title label in #B84C1E at the very top, followed by the section content.
Buttons (e.g. 'Next: Ingredients →') are solid #B84C1E rectangles with #FDF6EC text, no border-radius, 12px 28px padding, displayed as block centered elements at the bottom of each page.

page_specs.featured-recipe: Displays the recipe title 'Slow-Braised Short Rib with Smoked Rye Whiskey Glaze' in large 36px Georgia bold, followed by a one-paragraph recipe introduction describing the dish's origin in a Wyoming ranch kitchen. Shows three metadata rows — Prep Time: 30 min, Cook Time: 3 hr 45 min, Serves: 4 — each as a label-value pair in sage #7A8C6E label and charcoal value, separated by thin rules. A burnt-sienna button at the bottom reads 'Next: Ingredients →'.
page_specs.ingredients: Lists all ingredients under two headed groups — 'For the Short Ribs' and 'For the Rye Glaze' — each group heading in 20px Georgia bold #B84C1E. Ingredients are plain unstyled list items in body text, e.g. '2.5 lb bone-in beef short ribs', '3 tbsp smoked rye whiskey', '1 cup beef bone broth'. A horizontal rule separates the two groups. A burnt-sienna button at the bottom reads 'Next: Method →'.
page_specs.method: Presents the cooking steps as a numbered ordered list with six steps, each step starting with a bold action verb like 'Sear:', 'Deglaze:', 'Braise:' in charcoal, followed by a plain-text instruction sentence. Steps are separated by 16px of vertical space. A brief 'Active vs. Passive Time' note in sage italic sits below the final step. A burnt-sienna button at the bottom reads 'Next: Tips & Notes →'.
page_specs.tips-and-notes: Contains three distinct tip blocks — 'Choosing Your Cut', 'Whiskey Substitutions', and 'Make-Ahead Storage' — each with a bold sage #7A8C6E heading and two to three sentences of practical advice. Thin burnt-sienna rules separate each tip block. A wine-pairing note at the bottom suggests a Syrah from the Columbia Valley in italic body text. A burnt-sienna button reads 'Next: About the Cook →'.
page_specs.about-the-cook: A short biography of the fictional recipe author, Darlene Forthwright, a former line cook from Sheridan, Wyoming who now documents family ranch recipes. Three paragraphs: her background, her philosophy on slow cooking, and a closing invitation to write in with questions via a displayed email address ember.rye.kitchen@mail.com. No button on this final page; instead a centered closing mark — a small typographic flourish '— E&R —' in #B84C1E at the very bottom.
